title: When Agents Rule — per-turn game state
description: The JSON object handed to every model each turn (built by buildGameStateJSON in js/openai-ai.js). Everything is fog-limited: the model only sees what its own units/buildings have discovered. Coordinates are world units on a square map centred on (0,0). In multi-turn history mode, PAST turns are replayed as compact 'pastTurnRecap' objects (see $defs.pastTurnRecap) — the CURRENT full state is always the last message.

clock.description: What every other 'seconds' figure in this state is measured against. The match runs in real time and does NOT pause while you think, so durations elsewhere (buildSecondsRemaining, secondsUntilEnemyWins, secondsAgo, walk times) are wall-clock seconds, not turns.

averageSecondsBetweenTurns.description: How much real time has typically passed between your recent turns — your own thinking time included. It converts any duration into the number of DECISIONS you get while that thing runs: at 18, a 40-second build leaves you about two more turns before it completes. Those turns are not spent on it — construction, training, research and age advances continue on their own — so what they are worth is up to you. Absent on your first turn, before any interval has been observed. It reflects your RECENT turns, so it moves if your responses speed up or slow down.

epoch.description: Age progression. Costs come from the shared AGE_COSTS table — identical for every player type.

upgradeInProgress.description: null when no age-up is running. Do NOT re-issue upgrade_age while non-null.

maxPopulation.description: Raised by houses, up to populationHardCap.

populationHardCap.description: Hard ceiling (100); houses cannot raise maxPopulation past it.

recentEvents.description: Your battle report: up to 8 recent one-line events (losses, kills, raids), each prefixed with its age, e.g. "12s ago: LOSS: your house at (140, -80) — destroyed by egyptian".

bonuses.description: Only NON-DEFAULT multipliers appear (empty object = no active bonuses).

map.description: World geometry. The map is a square centred on (0,0); enemies can be anywhere inside bounds.

nodesLeftOnMap.description: How many resource nodes of each type are STILL on the map right now, across the whole world (e.g. {"food":312,"wood":690,"stone":37,"gold":18}). Compare with discoveredNodesOnMap — the ones YOU have actually found — to judge whether more scouting is worth it, and watch it fall to see the world running dry: a wood figure sliding toward zero is the signal to stop prospecting and commit to farms. Live rather than a starting snapshot, because a fixed figure goes stale and would have you hunting nodes that no longer exist. It is a total only: no positions, no indication of which nodes went or who took them.

yourBaseTiles.description: Which map tiles you occupy, keyed by the same A1-G7 label as exploration, with the number of your buildings in each (e.g. {"D1": 7, "G7": 1}). Covers a second base or one rebuilt elsewhere after the first fell; empty when you have no buildings left. Deliberately not a single averaged tile: a player holding D1 and G7 has a centroid in E4, where it owns nothing.

exploration.description: Percent (0-100) of each map tile you have ever seen, keyed by tile label: column letter A-G running west to east, then row number 1-7 running north to south ("C5" = third column, fifth row). Pass one of these labels as explore's "tile". map.yourBaseTile tells you which one you start in. A tile is ~114 units across and a scout sees ~15, so one pass raises a tile by only a few percent.

discoveredNodesOnMap.description: How many nodes of each type YOU have found and not yet seen emptied — discovered by sight, then remembered. Its counterpart is map.nodesLeftOnMap, which counts what exists across the WHOLE map: the gap between them is what is still out there unscouted, and whether closing it is worth a scout is your call. Counts only; for coordinates see nearestNodes.

nearestNodes.description: Where to actually send a worker: the food and wood nodes closest to EACH of your Town Centers (ten apiece, so a second base is listed too, not crowded out by the first), plus every stone and gold node you have found — those are scarce enough to list whole. This is a shortlist, not the limit of what you know: assign_workers accepts the coordinates of ANY node you have scouted, including one that dropped off here. "amount" is live while a node is in sight and otherwise the amount as you last saw it, so one a rival emptied out of your view keeps its remembered figure until you look again. Nodes deplete permanently.

battles.description: Fighting you could not watch, one entry per ENGAGEMENT (clustered by location), CUMULATIVE since it began — a fight outlasts several turns. Absent entirely when nothing is happening. Only engagements you took part in. Numbers only: 'their 2 heavy_cavalry dealt 1800 to my 3 archers' is the counter lesson, drawing it is your job.

ongoing.description: true while blows are still landing.

endedSecondsAgo.description: Only present once ongoing is false: how long ago the last blow landed. An entry is reported for two minutes after a fight ends, so this is what tells a finished skirmish from a stale one.

secondsElapsed.description: How long the fight LASTED (first blow to last), not how long ago it was — see endedSecondsAgo for that.

enemy.description: One entry per rival involved — three-way fights are not merged.

involved.description: Keyed by unit/building type — towers, drafted workers and priests all appear as themselves. Zero fields are omitted, so a priest row is just {n, healed}.

n.description: Distinct units of this type that fought or healed.

dmgBuildings.description: Damage dealt to BUILDINGS — never summed with dmgUnits, since infantry raze at 1.5x and ranged at 0.5x.

healed.description: HP restored (priests). Replaces damage for support units.

lost.description: Units of yours (or theirs) that died here, by type. Absent if none.

friendlyBuildings.description: Every building you own, with its live activity.

busy.description: true while producing, under construction, or hosting research/age-up.

activity.description: researching = hosting a TECH (see research.current); advancing_age = hosting the epoch upgrade (see epoch.upgradeInProgress). They are separate tasks and can run at the same time, in different buildings.

researchingTech.description: Tech id this building is researching. Only while activity is researching.

researchSecondsRemaining.description: Seconds until this building finishes its tech. Only while activity is researching.

advancingTo.description: Epoch this Town Center is advancing to. Only while activity is advancing_age.

ageSecondsRemaining.description: Seconds until the epoch upgrade completes. Only while activity is advancing_age.

producing.description: Unit type in production, else null.

secondsUntilYouWin.description: Your Wonder only: seconds of holding still required before it wins the match for you. null while it is under construction. The same clock your rivals read as secondsUntilEnemyWins — it resets to the full duration if the Wonder falls.

revealedToAll.description: Your Wonder only, always true: a finished or half-built Wonder is shown to EVERY rival regardless of fog of war, including its coordinates and this countdown. Unlike your other buildings it cannot be hidden, so its location is a decision made in the open.

food.description: Farms only: food currently stocked in the farm.

buildings.description: Aggregate of friendlyBuildings, for at-a-glance rebalancing.

enemyBuildings.description: Every enemy building you have EVER discovered (buildings are static, so they are remembered). Wonders ignore fog — always listed. Attack by coordinates or pass the exact `id` as attack_target's params.targetId.

visible.description: true = in sight now; false = remembered last-seen location (still a valid target).

secondsUntilEnemyWins.description: Wonders only: time until this wonder WINS for its owner. null while under construction.

friendlyUnits.description: Your units. Each carries an "id" you can pass in unitIds to move_units or attack_target to address it exactly; ids are never reused, so one that has vanished means that unit died. Fog-limited only in the sense that these are all yours.

workers.description: Live tally of what your villagers are doing — use it to rebalance the economy at a glance. Gathering is a round trip (walk out, gather, carry home), so a worker on wood is on wood for the whole cycle and onWood counts all of them, wherever they are in it.

moving.description: In transit with no resolved job yet.

onFood.description: Workers whose job is food, at ANY point of the cycle: walking out, gathering, or carrying home. Reassigning a worker who happens to be carrying a load destroys it — but which ones those are changes every few seconds, far faster than a turn, so it is not something to plan from here. assign_workers' allowSpill decides it at the moment the order runs.

enemyUnits.description: Enemy units CURRENTLY in your sight (units move, so old positions are not kept). The `id` is a target handle for attack_target — ids track a moving unit better than stale coordinates.

research.description: Tech tree status. Only ONE research at a time; never re-research something in `researched`.

researchAt.description: Building type that hosts this research (e.g. town_center, market).

unlockedContent.description: Buildings whose unlock tech you have already researched. This is a HISTORY of what you unlocked, not a list of what you may build — for that use buildableStructures.

trainableUnits.description: The vocabulary for train_unit's "unitType": every unit your civilization can EVER train, nested building -> age -> entries, where the age is the earliest one at which that unit can be trained. Units you cannot reach yet are listed on purpose, so you can plan toward them — blockedBy says what stands in the way of each, and is empty for the ones you could order right now. Each entry's "id" is the bare unit id, ready to pass as "unitType" unchanged. The Town Center hosts "worker" — villagers are trained with train_unit like everything else. Includes your civ's unique units, and omits both standard units your civ does not field and any host building your civ can never construct. A category (infantry/ranged/cavalry/support) is NOT valid here — those work only in the "units" parameter of move_units and attack_target.

buildableStructures.description: What your civilization can EVER build, and the vocabulary for build_structure's "buildingType". If a type is absent (e.g. stable for some civs), your civ does not have it — do not keep trying. Your Wonder is listed here too, as type "wonder".

requiredAge.description: Earliest age at which this civilization can build it — already adjusted for a unlock tech that arrives later than the building's own age.

isWonder.description: Present and true on the single Wonder entry. Building it and holding it for gameStats.wonderRequired seconds wins the match.

builtAs.description: Wonder only: the civilization-specific id the finished Wonder carries in friendlyBuildings (akropolis, pyramid, firetemple, shrine). You still BUILD it by passing type "wonder".

recentLosses.description: Buildings of YOURS destroyed in the last two minutes. Absent entirely when you have lost nothing. A building simply disappears from friendlyBuildings when it falls, so without this a structure you were told you had started — a Wonder above all — would vanish with no explanation. Losses older than the window drop off; a repeated entry means it happened again, not that it is still happening.

wonder.description: Present (true) only if the lost building was your Wonder — your win condition is gone and its countdown has reset.

to.description: Which rival destroyed it, when that is known. Absent if it fell to something unattributed.

underAttack.description: YOUR units/buildings that took damage in the last few seconds — defend now (attack the attackerAt position).

enemyWonders.description: Rival Wonders (always visible, even through fog). A complete one WINS for its owner in secondsUntilEnemyWins — existential threat.

wonderRequired.description: Seconds a finished Wonder must be held to win (600 by default). Your own progress against it is secondsUntilYouWin on your Wonder in friendlyBuildings; a rival's is secondsUntilEnemyWins in threats.enemyWonders.

opponents.description: Every rival (rule-based ones and, in campaign, the human with id 'player'). civilization and age are ALWAYS present (epochs are public). units/buildings counts appear only once this player has DISCOVERED that rival (seen any of its units or buildings); until then discovered=false and the count fields are absent.

blockedBy.description: What stands between you and ordering this RIGHT NOW. Empty means nothing does. Listed structural gates first, price last — the same order the harness reports them if you try anyway. "pop" appears on units when you are at the population cap; buildings never consume population and never carry it. Absent on purpose: whether a trainer or research host is busy this instant, which changes several times while you are thinking and so could not be reported honestly here.

pastTurnRecap.description: NOT part of the live state: in multi-turn history mode, each PAST turn is replayed to the model as this condensed snapshot (flagged by pastTurnRecap:true). Field names mirror the live state; long per-entity arrays are replaced by counts. The current, complete state is always the LAST message.

workers.description: Same keys as the live workers tally.
